extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won from a four-rider breakaway that held off the peloton by seconds, surviving GEE Derek's late attack inside 300m to go.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL Remco's COVID withdrawal forced INEOS to defend pink unexpectedly, revealing organizational depth with four top-10 riders.
Four-rider break survived because sprinter teams lost domestiques to crashes and clothing changes in horrific weather conditions.
MILAN Jonathan attempted dual role at intermediate sprint and finale, burning matches that compromised peloton chase organization.
GEE Derek attacked twice inside final 1500m but held acceleration too long, allowing CORT Magnus to conserve sprint.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: inherited race lead and successfully defended despite chaotic weather transitioning from domestique to leadership role
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: abandoned after feeling ill and getting distanced early, losing GC contention before decisive mountain stages
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: lost 10 minutes and top-10 GC after losing contact during clothing changes, exposing poor positioning discipline
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: completed Grand Tour stage win trilogy by conserving energy behind GEE's premature attacks in breakaway finish
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: crashed during clothing change chase and never regained peloton contact despite Movistar support riding all day
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: neutral: won intermediate sprint over MATTHEWS but compromised team chase organization by attempting double duty
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: successfully chased back after being distanced, demonstrating resilience despite reduced sprint train support
rider_signals.DUNBAR Eddie: positive: moved into top nine GC as INEOS placed fourth rider in top ten after rivals' misfortunes
